justification: All standard ADR sections present (Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives Considered). Crash-recovery lock behavior explicitly described in Consequences (positive section: 'if the coordinator process dies, the connection drops and PostgreSQL releases the lock automatically'). All three alternatives listed: Redis SETNX, ZooKeeper, and custom heartbeat table. Context clearly explains the multi-node expansion problem and why coordination is needed. No invented information — the four options discussed all trace back to MJ's input. ADR is fully readable as a standalone document.

observations.text: Excellent structure with all required ADR sections plus a useful Implementation Notes section
Crash-recovery behavior clearly explained: connection drop triggers automatic lock release
Goes beyond the minimum by distinguishing the stall case (process hangs with open connection) from the crash case — adds genuine operational value
All three alternatives presented in a clean comparison table with clear rejection rationale
Context section thoroughly explains the multi-node expansion problem and the single-coordinator requirement
No invented alternatives or fabricated context — stays faithful to source material
The commentary notes after the ADR add useful meta-context without polluting the ADR itself

Lists four alternatives in the Context section (including pg_try_advisory_lock itself) which slightly muddies the distinction between the chosen approach and the rejected alternatives, though the Alternatives table correctly lists only the three rejected options

agent_output_excerpt:

# ADR-001: PostgreSQL Advisory Locks for Nightly Batch Job Coordination

## Status
Accepted

## Date
2026-03-17

## Context

The nightly reconciliation job was previously designed for a single-node deployment
with no distributed coordination. As the system expands to multiple nodes, exactly-once
execution per night must be enforced — allowing multiple nodes to run the batch
simultaneously would produce duplicate work and corrupt reconciliation state.

A coordination mechanism is requ
